Title: Beet mosaic virus expression of a betalain transcription factor allows visual virus tracking in Beta vulgaris

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: visual pigmentation; enables noninvasive virus tracking in planta
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf tissue, mainly lower leaf surface; vacuole
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cleaves L-DOPA to form the betalamic acid precursor in betalain biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms key chromophore precursor for target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expression significantly increased over time in BtMV-MYB-infected systemic leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: higher BvDODA transcript abundance is associated with visible red betalain accumulation in BtMV-MYB-infected white beet leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BvDODA transcript level increased over time in BtMV-MYB-infected beets. 2. Its induction lagged behind BvMYB1 up-regulation at 6 dpi. 3. Expression strongly correlated with BtMV-MYB viral load and visible betalain pigmentation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR time-course; correlation analysis with viral load; pigment spectroscopy
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: infection with recombinant Beet mosaic virus expressing BvMYB1 (BtMV-MYB) under greenhouse growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: viral polyprotein expression from BtMV cDNA clone driven by CaMV 2x35S promoter
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: can serve as a downstream marker gene for induced betalain production and visual reporter systems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper measured transcript induction only; direct enzymatic assay was not performed.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: visual pigmentation; enables noninvasive virus tracking in planta
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf tissue, mainly lower leaf surface; vacuole
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes oxidation steps required for red betalain formation in the beet betalain pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expression significantly increased over time in BtMV-MYB-infected systemic leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: higher BvCYP76AD1 transcript abundance accompanies and supports visible red pigment accumulation in BtMV-MYB-infected white beet leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BvCYP76AD1 expression was only slightly increased at 6 dpi and not significantly different from BtMV1228. 2. It became significantly up-regulated over time in BtMV-MYB-infected plants. 3. Its expression highly correlated with BtMV-MYB accumulation and betalain pigmentation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR time-course; correlation analysis with viral load; pigment spectroscopy
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: prerequisite gene for betalain expression according to discussion; key control role in permitting pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: infection with recombinant Beet mosaic virus expressing BvMYB1 (BtMV-MYB) under greenhouse growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: viral polyprotein expression from BtMV cDNA clone driven by CaMV 2x35S promoter
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: important prerequisite locus to consider when deploying visual betalain reporters in breeding populations
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion notes the R-locus responsible for red hypocotyl colour mapped to BvCYP76AD1 and is a prerequisite to express betalains; loss of pigmentation does not necessarily indicate virus resistance.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: visual pigmentation; enables naked-eye virus tracking and potential high-throughput phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: systemically infected leaves, especially oldest leaves and lower leaf surface; stored in vacuole
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates endogenous betalain biosynthesis genes BvDODA and BvCYP76AD1, increasing their transcript levels over time
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: decisive activator sufficient to trigger the endogenous beet betalain pathway when expressed from virus
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: heterologous expression from recombinant Beet mosaic virus BtMV-MYB
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: small quantities of intracellular BvMYB1 transcript can be found in white beet even without BtMV-MYB infection
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: significantly up-regulated from 6 dpi onward in BtMV-MYB-infected systemic leaves; highest correlation with viral load
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: induces red betalain accumulation in white beet leaves, allowing naked-eye tracking of virus spread
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Viral expression of BvMYB1 activated the endogenous betalain biosynthesis pathway in Beta vulgaris. 2. BvMYB1 transcript was significantly increased by 6 dpi, before visible pigmentation and before strong induction of BvDODA and BvCYP76AD1. 3. BtMV-MYB infection caused progressive visible red pigmentation and BvMYB1 expression showed a very high correlation with viral load (r^2=0.97). 4. The inserted BvMYB1 reporter remained stable over five passages and enabled noninvasive visual virus tracking.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: virus-mediated heterologous expression; visible pigment phenotype; betalain extraction/spectroscopy; RT-qPCR of BvMYB1 and downstream genes; viral load correlation analysis; serial passage stability assay
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 2x35S promoter driving the BtMV full-length cDNA clone
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: BtMV-MYB infection in greenhouse-grown Beta vulgaris; comparison with BtMV1228 and mock controls
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: insertion of BvMYB1 into the viral genome reduced viral replication and slowed spread, especially early infection; ~2-day delay between symptom onset and visible pigmentation; uneven pigmentation due to inhomogeneous virus distribution; possible partial RNA silencing cannot be excluded
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful as a visual reporter for whole-plant virus tracking and potentially high-throughput screening of disease severity or tolerance; deployment in breeding requires caution because pigmentation depends on intact betalain pathway genes such as BvCYP76AD1 and absence of pigmentation does not automatically indicate resistance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Anthocyanin-producing Nicotiana benthamiana did not accumulate red pigments after BtMV-MYB inoculation, highlighting host-pathway specificity. The reporter is indirect because pigment appears after a delay and vacuolar storage may limit tracking in some cell types.
